conversation.intent: Long-running coding, testing, support, release, CI-hardening, V14 compatibility, and unreleased PDF paste support thread for Foundry Paste Eater.

user_preferences: Commit and push serially, never in parallel.
Prefer strong verification and full release passes.
Keep docs, tests, and runtime aligned.
Prefer practical GM-facing UX and sensible defaults.
Do not loosen compatibility claims without validation.
Avoid nested ternaries where control flow clarity matters.
Avoid brittle Foundry editor helper behavior that mutates wrapper DOM instead of the real editable field.

repo_state.note: Non-handoff repo state is intentionally dirty with unreleased PDF paste support work on top of 96868de (Release 1.3.1). No new release has been created for this PDF work. Handoff snapshot files under handoff/ remain excluded from repo-state parity checks.

release_state.post_release_note: The release is published, the release commit CI run passed, and no functional post-release blocker is known from the V14 validation pass.

latest_manual_v14_exercises: Cross-editor selection fallback inserted blocked direct-media URL text into V14 ProseMirror chat without altering the foreign contenteditable selection target.
Chat rerender via ui.chat.render(true) preserved a descendant contenteditable editor and the text fallback path still worked.
Scene Paste Media prompt in always mode accepted an SVG paste, created a tile, closed successfully, and was cleaned up afterward.

latest_handoff_validation.status: passed after the unreleased PDF support handoff refresh

latest_pdf_development_pass.command: env -u FOUNDRY_PASSWORD FOUNDRY_URL=http://127.0.0.1:30014 FOUNDRY_DOCKER_CONTAINER=foundry-v14-dev npm run verify:release
latest_pdf_development_pass.skipped: Opt-in S3 bucket integration smoke skipped because S3 env was not configured.
latest_pdf_development_pass.note: This was a full local development validation for unreleased PDF paste support, not a new release.

decisions.summary: The module now supports Foundry V13 and is verified through Foundry V14.359 with manifest maximum 14.
Do not further loosen compatibility claims without another real Foundry validation pass.
V14 chat fallback insertion should resolve the actual contenteditable editor and recreate the range when the current selection is outside that editor.
Playwright chat reset helpers should clear the resolved descendant editable, not blank ProseMirror wrapper textContent or innerHTML.
Canvas-bound GIFs are rasterized to PNG for reliable Foundry rendering; chat GIFs remain animated.
New pasted tokens create backing Actors by default.
New actor-backed pasted tokens default to actorLink true.
Default empty-canvas media target is active layer.
Canvas text paste defaults to scene notes.
Selected token image paste defaults to Ask each time.
Unless a test is explicitly validating logging settings or quiet-mode behavior, unit and smoke verification should run with the module's most verbose logging configuration enabled.
PDF paste support uses world Journal PDF pages as the canonical v1 artifact, not live compendium writes or file-link-only references.

PDF Journal entries created from chat or empty-canvas paste are shared for players, but appending a PDF to an existing selected scene Note preserves that Journal entry ownership to avoid exposing unrelated pages.
External PDF URL fallback is allowed only for clearly PDF-like URLs when browser-side download is blocked; ambiguous URLs remain normal text unless download proves they are PDFs.
No new release has been created for the PDF work; release_state remains anchored to published version 1.3.1.

implemented_changes: GM-only Readiness & Support panel
GM-only Uploaded Media Audit panel
Support bundle export and read-only support API
Release hardening with typecheck, verify:bundle, and verify:release
Runtime and test architecture refactor
Smoke scene isolation
Finder/native media paste hardening
Chat focus restoration after chat paste
Recommended-defaults and support/audit UI polish
Actor-linked token creation by default for actor-backed pasted tokens
Configurable pasted-token actor types and token-creation dialog
Canonical handoff files, schema validation, and handoff protocol tooling
Handoff validation decoupled from verify:release and converted to repo-relative non-handoff repo-state checks
Handoff history append-only enforcement against tracked HEAD content and relaxed narrative-field minimum schema
Local Playwright CLI debug artifacts are ignored through the .playwright-cli/ gitignore rule
Foundry V14.359 manifest verification for release 1.3.1
V14 ProseMirror chat fallback insertion fix with stale-selection re-anchoring
V14 Playwright chat helper fix so clearChatInputs clears descendant editables instead of destroying ProseMirror wrapper DOM
Regression coverage for fallback insertion when another contenteditable owns the live selection
GitHub release 1.3.1 with accurate V14 compatibility release notes and assets
Unreleased PDF paste support as a new document content kind backed by world Journal PDF pages
PDF detection from local files, direct .pdf URLs, uri-list payloads, HTML links, and plain text
PDF-safe upload and remote URL resolution under the pdf upload context without image/video media coercion
Chat PDF cards with UUID Journal links and best-effort preview/icon fallback
Canvas PDF scene Notes and selected-note PDF page append behavior, with selected existing Journal ownership preserved
Focused PDF Journal page src field handling without hijacking unsupported editable fields
PDF unit coverage, PDF Playwright smoke coverage, PDF fixture, docs, support/audit/readiness updates, and regenerated runtime artifact
Review fix: selected-note PDF append preflights linked JournalEntry update rights before upload so blocked targets do not leave orphaned PDF uploads
open_threads: Possible future setting: only allow token replacement for tokens owned by the user.
Possible future feature: scene drag/drop from Finder or Explorer.
Possible future experiment: optional GIF to WebM conversion for canvas uploads.
Post-release watch: monitor 1.3.1 user feedback, CI, and release asset health; no known functional blocker is open from this validation pass.
PDF support is implemented and fully validated locally but remains unreleased; review, commit, and release planning are still pending.
Possible future PDF enhancements: compendium curation/export, deduplication, page-range links, stronger thumbnail generation through bundled PDF.js, and text extraction.
Possible future Codex Skills or multi-agent review workflow for specialized review perspectives after the PDF review path is closed.

summary_lineage.note: Earlier work was preserved informally through assistant close-out summaries after major implementation steps.
A later system carry-forward summary captured the release-pass state and pending files before the final release work.
This JSON + Markdown handoff is intended to become the canonical restart context for future agents.
A schema, validator, npm script, verify-release gate, and AGENTS protocol were added to enforce future handoff quality.

Handoff validation was decoupled from verify:release, switched to repo-relative file references, changed to track non-handoff repo-state parity only, and now enforces append-only history updates once tracked while keeping narrative handoff fields recommended rather than schema-mandatory.

The tracked handoff snapshot was re-reviewed against the live clean main branch and updated to carry forward the current maintainer expectations around verbose diagnostics and ignored local Playwright CLI artifacts.
This refresh re-anchors the handoff to the 1.3.1 release commit, records real Foundry V14.359 validation, and preserves the resolved V14 ProseMirror chat reset and fallback-insertion review context.

This refresh records the unreleased PDF paste support implementation, the two PDF review fixes, and the full local Foundry V14 validation pass while keeping release_state anchored to published version 1.3.1.

rules: Keep the last 5-10 user asks verbatim or near-verbatim.
Append new milestones to handoff/history.ndjson rather than rewriting chronology from memory.
Append to summary_lineage rather than replacing it.
Preserve facts, decisions, preferences, open questions, and risks as distinct categories.

resume_prompt: Resume from main at commit 96868de (Release 1.3.1) with a deliberately dirty non-handoff worktree containing unreleased PDF paste support. Do not assume a new release was created: release_state remains 1.3.1. Start by checking git status and reviewing src/paste/pdf-workflows.js, src/notes.js, src/clipboard.js, src/chat.js, and the PDF unit/smoke tests. The PDF work passed lint, unit, typecheck, bundle parity, and verify:release against the existing foundry-v14-dev container at http://127.0.0.1:30014 with 94 Playwright smoke tests passed and 1 S3 smoke skipped. If you edit handoff files again, append to handoff/history.ndjson and run npm run handoff:validate.
